description_long: This flower features three prominent, elongated upper petals with a creamy yellow base and brownish-orange speckles, radiating outward in a slightly asymmetrical arrangement. The lower section displays two broad, rounded petals with a striking magenta-pink coloration and a central white area, creating a vivid contrast. At the center, a small, intricate structure resembling a column or lip is visible, with pink and white hues and a delicate, almost sculpted appearance. The petals have a soft, velvety texture and appear slightly thickened, suggesting a waxy or fleshy consistency typical of some orchid species. No stem or leaves are visible, and the flower appears solitary, not part of a cluster. The overall form is bilateral, with a clear top and bottom orientation and a visually striking color pattern.
description: A striking flower with three speckled yellow upper petals and two broad magenta-pink lower petals, featuring a detailed central column. The petals are thick and velvety, with a bilateral arrangement and vivid color contrast.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three speckled upper petals and two broad magenta-pink lower petals, with the central column clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness and curvature of the petals, with the layered structure of the flower evident.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full spread of the three upper petals and the rounded shape of the lower petals, with the central column partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the petal thickness and the way the lower petals curve forward, partially obscuring the central column.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged bilaterally, with three upper petals radiating outward and two lower petals forming a broad, rounded base. The arrangement is not perfectly radial, showing bilateral symmetry with a distinct top and bottom. The petals overlap slightly at their bases, and the central column is prominent.
